author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Storage spec v2.7 → v2.8 ships. `IssueRecord` gains
`priority: Option<u8>` between `type` and `labels`; new
`Op::SetPriority` carries `Jjf-Priority:` (integer string or
empty for clear). Create-time emission, `UpdateFields::priority`
three-way plumbing, `Storage::set_priority` scalar mutator, all
gated by `validate_priority` (0..=4). `list_ready` primary sort
key is now `priority` nulls-last via the
`priority_sort_key((u8, u8))` tuple — `Some(0)` < `Some(4)` <
`None`.

CLI surface: `-p/--priority` on `new` / `update` / `ls` /
`ready`, `--unset-priority` on `update`. `jjf show` plain-text
adds a `priority:` line; `--json` carries the field via serde.
Row format becomes
`<id>\t<status>\t<priority>\t<type>\t<title>` (priority renders
`P0`..`P4` or `-`). New error kind `invalid_priority` (exit 2)
mirrors `body_too_large`'s envelope shape.

`docs/storage-format.md` v2.8 changelog + table updates; `docs/cli-json.md` v2.10 → v2.11 changelog documents every new flag, the row shape, the sort change, and the envelope.

# Recommendation

Land. Spec is backwards-compatible: v2.7 readers tolerate v2.8
records (unknown-field serde-default) and `set-priority` ops
(unknown-op tolerance per §5.2). Out-of-range trailer payloads
fall through the same unknown-op-shape tolerance.

# Confidence

High. 595/595 workspace tests green (was 587; +8 priority
tests covering round-trip, default null, set+unset, out-of-
range rejection, ready sort, ls filter, row format, trailer
round-trip). Smoke verification end-to-end confirmed: P0
sorts first, unset clears to null, `--priority 5` exits 2 via
clap's range check.

# Open follow-ups

- Storage-side `ReadyFilter::priorities` would mirror
  `labels` / `types` and move the CLI's `retain` into
  `Storage::list_ready`. Filed-worthy if a non-CLI consumer
  (MCP, scripted batch) wants priority-filtered ready sets.
- Hygiene pass to migrate existing `p0` / `p1` labels in this
  planner to first-class `priority`. Explicitly out-of-scope
  here; file separately when the asterinas import lands.
